backup.title: Backup
backup.icloudSyncDesc: Automatically saves a backup to your iCloud folder when you leave the app.
backup.icloudTip: To enable iCloud sync go to Settings > Your name > iCloud > Apps Using iCloud and enable HuertoTracker.
backup.exportDesc: Save the file to Files, Drive, or share it.
backup.importDesc2: Restore from a .json file. Replaces all data.
backup.footerNote: Backup does not include photos (stored in device gallery) or subscription status.
backup.proDesc: Automatic iCloud sync is available on the Pro plan.
backup.importDesc:
This will replace ALL current data with data from the backup file. The app will need to restart.

Continue?
backup.restoredDesc: Close and reopen the app for changes to take effect.
backup.exportError: Could not export backup. Please try again.

notifications.how2Desc: Suggested crops are calculated based on your climate zone in Spain.
notifications.how3Desc: Alerts are scheduled on the device. No internet needed.
notifications.noZoneWarning: Complete onboarding to activate personalised zone alerts.
notifications.frostAlertsDesc: Get a notification when temperatures below 2°C are forecast in the coming days.
notifications.frostNoProvince: Set your province in the garden settings to enable frost alerts.

recommendation.frutos: Good moon for harvesting and planting tomatoes, cucumbers and fruits.
recommendation.hojas: Ideal for sowing lettuces, spinach and aromatic herbs.
recommendation.raices: Perfect for carrots, radishes and root vegetables.
recommendation.flores: Transplant flowers and aromatic plants today.
recommendation.descanso: New moon day. Rest the soil and plan ahead.

weather.wateringHot: 🌡️ Intense heat ({{temp}}°C) — water early in the morning.

gardenType.balconDesc: Growing in containers outdoors. The calendar will filter crops suitable for containers.
gardenType.macetaDesc: Growing indoors or on windowsills. The calendar will filter crops suitable for indoor growing.

entryEdit.title: Edit entry
entryEdit.deleteDesc: This entry will be permanently deleted.

tomate.name: Tomato
tomate.tips: Remove suckers regularly to focus energy on the fruits. Water at the base, never on the leaves.

pimiento.name: Pepper
pimiento.tips: Needs warm nights to set fruit well. On terraces, place it against a south-facing wall.

berenjena.name: Aubergine
berenjena.tips: The most heat-demanding crop in the nightshade family. Harvest before the skin loses its shine.

fresa.name: Strawberry
fresa.tips: Plant in autumn to harvest in spring. Mulch with straw to retain moisture and prevent fruit touching the soil.

lechuga.name: Lettuce
lechuga.tips: Stagger sowings every 2–3 weeks for a continuous supply. In heat it bolts quickly; choose heat-resistant varieties.

espinaca.name: Spinach
espinaca.tips: Cool-season crop. Harvest outer leaves and the heart will keep producing for several weeks.

acelga.name: Swiss Chard
acelga.tips: Very productive and hardy. Cut from the outside leaving the heart; one plant can last more than a year.

rucula.name: Rocket
rucula.tips: Grows in 4 weeks. Gets very peppery and bolts in heat; sow in partial shade in summer.

canonigos.name: Lamb's Lettuce
canonigos.tips: Ideal autumn-winter crop to keep the garden productive in the cold season. Sow densely.

zanahoria.name: Carrot
zanahoria.tips: Needs deep, loose soil without stones for the root to grow straight. Thin to 8 cm when seedlings are 5 cm tall.

rabano.name: Radish
rabano.tips: The fastest crop in the garden, ideal for filling gaps. Harvest promptly or it becomes woody and overly peppery.

remolacha.name: Beetroot
remolacha.tips: Each seed is a fruit containing several seeds; thin to 10 cm leaving the most vigorous seedling.

patata.name: Potato
patata.tips: Earth up soil around the stem as it grows for more tubers. Harvest when the plant yellows.

nabo.name: Turnip
nabo.tips: Rustic autumn crop. Harvest small (5–7 cm) for best flavour; larger ones become bitter and fibrous.

judia-verde.name: Green Bean
judia-verde.tips: Harvest frequently while pods still bend; letting them mature exhausts the plant. Climbing varieties need 1.5 m support.

guisante.name: Pea
guisante.tips: Cool-season crop. Provide support from the start. Harvest when pods are well-filled but still green.

haba.name: Broad Bean
haba.tips: Sow in autumn for greater yield. Pinch the main stem when first fruits appear to control black aphid.

brocoli.name: Broccoli
brocoli.tips: Harvest the central head before it flowers. Plants will keep producing small side shoots for weeks.

coliflor.name: Cauliflower
coliflor.tips: When the curd forms, fold the outer leaves over it to blanch and protect it from the sun.

col.name: Cabbage
col.tips: Very frost-hardy. Plant in firm soil and earth up around the stem so it doesn't topple in the wind.

kale.name: Kale
kale.tips: Harvest lower and outer leaves; the plant keeps growing from the top. Cold improves its flavour, making it sweeter.

calabacin.name: Courgette
calabacin.tips: A single plant can yield 3–4 kg per week. Harvest at 15–20 cm so the plant doesn't exhaust itself.

pepino.name: Cucumber
pepino.tips: Train on a trellis to save space on terraces. Harvest daily when production is at its peak.

calabaza.name: Pumpkin
calabaza.tips: Needs lots of space; on small terraces use small-fruited or climbing varieties. Ready when the stem dries.

melon.name: Melon
melon.tips: Prune to 2–3 fruits per plant for large, sweet melons. Reduce watering once fruits have set to concentrate sugar.

sandia.name: Watermelon
sandia.tips: Tap the fruit gently: a hollow sound means ripe. The tendril closest to the fruit should be dry at harvest.

albahaca.name: Basil
albahaca.tips: Pinch to encourage branching and prevent bolting. The best tomato companion: repels whitefly and aphids.

perejil.name: Parsley
perejil.tips: Slow to germinate (3–4 weeks). Soak seeds 24 h before sowing to speed it up. Cut from the outside, leaving the heart.

cilantro.name: Coriander
cilantro.tips: Bolts quickly in heat; sow in partial shade or in staggered sowings every 3 weeks for continuous production.

romero.name: Rosemary
romero.tips: Hardy perennial. Better to propagate by cuttings in spring than from seed. Does not tolerate waterlogging.

tomillo.name: Thyme
tomillo.tips: Mediterranean herb that withstands drought well. Prune after flowering to keep it compact and productive.

menta.name: Mint
menta.tips: Very invasive: always plant in a pot or with an underground barrier. Prefers partial shade in summer.

ajo.name: Garlic
ajo.tips: Plant individual cloves tip-up, 5 cm deep. When half the leaves dry out, it's time to harvest.

cebolla.name: Onion
cebolla.tips: Bend the leaves when they fall naturally and leave the bulb to cure in the ground for 2 weeks. Store cool and dry.

puerro.name: Leek
puerro.tips: Transplant into deep holes and earth up gradually to blanch the stem. Stays in the ground all autumn-winter; harvest as needed.

identify.title: Identify pest
identify.subtitle: Photograph the affected part of your plant
identify.healthyDesc: No pests or diseases were detected
identify.proOnlyDesc: AI identification is available on the Pro plan.
identify.freeCreditDesc: Try AI once for free. After that you'll need the Pro plan.
identify.freeCreditUsedDesc: Upgrade to Pro to identify pests and diseases without limits.
identify.errorDesc: Could not analyze the image. Please try again.
